# PocketBase: Auto-Compute Word Count and Reading Time (Go Event Hooks)

## Background

You are extending a PocketBase v0.31.0 application written in Go that hosts a small blog backend. A starter project lives at `/home/user/myproject` and already includes:

* A `go.mod` file that pins `github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase v0.31.0`.
* A `main.go` entry point that constructs the PocketBase app and starts it (you must extend it).
* A `migrations/` package that programmatically creates a base collection named `articles` with the following fields:
  * `title` (text, required)
  * `content` (text)
  * `word_count` (number, integer)
  * `reading_time_minutes` (number, integer)

Your job is to plug in PocketBase event hooks so that whenever an `articles` record is created or updated through the REST API, the server automatically derives the `word_count` and `reading_time_minutes` values from the submitted `content` field.

## Requirements

* Implement Go event hooks for the `articles` collection that run on both record create and record update REST requests.
* The hooks must set `word_count` to the number of whitespace-separated words found in the submitted `content` value (an empty or whitespace-only content yields 0 words).
* The hooks must set `reading_time_minutes` to `ceil(word_count / 200)` (so 0 words -> 0 minutes, 1-200 words -> 1 minute, 201-400 words -> 2 minutes, etc.).
* The computed values must overwrite anything the client supplied for `word_count` or `reading_time_minutes`, so clients cannot bypass the calculation.
* The hook chain must continue normally so the record is persisted; if you stop the chain the record will never be saved.
* Provision a superuser using the `superuser upsert` subcommand of the compiled binary, then start the server on `0.0.0.0:8090`.

## Implementation Hints

* Use the PocketBase v0.31 hook API (`BindFunc`) and remember to call `e.Next()` from your handler so the request continues through the middleware chain.
* `OnRecordCreateRequest("articles")` and `OnRecordUpdateRequest("articles")` are the relevant hooks for REST traffic.
* `e.Record.GetString("content")` returns the submitted text; `e.Record.Set(...)` writes the derived fields back onto the record before it is saved.
* The standard library `strings.Fields` plus `math.Ceil` are enough to compute the word count and the rounded-up reading time.
* Build the binary with `go build -o app .` and then run `./app superuser upsert admin@example.com SuperSecret123` to create the admin account before `./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: cd /home/user/myproject && ./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090
* Port: 8090
* The superuser `admin@example.com` (password `SuperSecret123`) must exist so the verifier can authenticate via `POST /api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password`.
* The `articles` collection (already provisioned by the bundled migration) must remain available with its `word_count` and `reading_time_minutes` integer fields.
* API behavior:
  * `POST /api/collections/articles/records` with a JSON body containing `title` and `content` must return `201 Created`, and the response body must include `word_count` equal to the number of whitespace-separated words in `content`, and `reading_time_minutes` equal to `ceil(word_count / 200)`.
  * `PATCH /api/collections/articles/records/{id}` with a new `content` value must return `200 OK`, and the response body must reflect the recomputed `word_count` and `reading_time_minutes` for the new content.
  * Client-supplied `word_count` or `reading_time_minutes` values in the request body must be overwritten by the hook (the response must reflect the values derived from `content`).
I'll start by exploring the project structure to understand the existing code.

todos.content: Implement event hooks for word_count and reading_time_minutes in main.go
Build the binary with go build
Provision superuser admin@example.com
Start the server and verify it works

part.text: Server is running. Let me verify the API behavior by testing the create and update endpoints.

input.command: curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/articles/records -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"title":"Test Article","content":"hello world this is a test"}' | python3 -m json.tool

part.text: Need to authenticate first. Let me get an auth token.

input.command: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"identity":"admin@example.com","password":"SuperSecret123"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])") && echo "Token: $TOKEN" && curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/articles/records -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"title":"Test Article","content":"hello world this is a test article with ten words total"}' | python3 -m json.tool

output.output:
Token: REDACTED
{
    "collectionId": "pbc_4287850865",
    "collectionName": "articles",
    "content": "hello world this is a test article with ten words total",
    "id": "aatmqz6symy5h2x",
    "reading_time_minutes": 1,
    "title": "Test Article",
    "word_count": 11
}

part.text: Create works correctly: 11 words → word_count=11, reading_time_minutes=ceil(11/200)=1. Now let me test the update (PATCH) and also test that client-supplied values get overwritten.

input.command: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"identity":"admin@example.com","password":"SuperSecret123"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])") && curl -s -X PATCH http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/articles/records/aatmqz6symy5h2x -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"content":"shorter content"}' | python3 -m json.tool

part.text: Update works: "shorter content" → 2 words, reading_time=ceil(2/200)=1. Now let me test that client-supplied values get overwritten.

input.command: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"identity":"admin@example.com","password":"SuperSecret123"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])") && echo "=== CREATE with spoofed values ===" && curl -s -X POST http://0.0.0.0:8090/api/collections/articles/records -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"title":"Spoofed","content":"one two three","word_count":99999,"reading_time_minutes":99999}' | python3 -m json.tool

output.output:
=== CREATE with spoofed values ===
{
    "collectionId": "pbc_4287850865",
    "collectionName": "articles",
    "content": "one two three",
    "id": "hp9hyzu4twj0afl",
    "reading_time_minutes": 1,
    "title": "Spoofed",
    "word_count": 3
}

part.text: Client-supplied values (99999) are correctly overwritten with the computed values (3 words, 1 minute). All tests pass.

todos.content: Implement event hooks for word_count and reading_time_minutes in main.go
Build the binary with go build
Provision superuser admin@example.com
Start the server and verify it works

input.result:
The PocketBase application is now running with auto-computed word count and reading time hooks.

## What was implemented

**`main.go`** — Added a shared event hook handler `computeReadingStats` that:
- Extracts the `content` field from the record
- Splits it on whitespace using `strings.Fields` to count words
- Computes `reading_time_minutes` as `ceil(word_count / 200)` (0 words → 0 minutes)
- Overwrites both `word_count` and `reading_time_minutes` on the record
- Calls `e.Next()` so the request chain continues and the record is persisted

The hook is registered on both `OnRecordCreateRequest("articles")` and `OnRecordUpdateRequest("articles")`.

## Verification results

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| `POST /api/collections/articles/records` with `"hello world this is a test article with ten words total"` (11 words) | `word_count: 11`, `reading_time_minutes: 1` ✅ |
| `PATCH /api/collections/articles/records/{id}` with `"shorter content"` (2 words) | `word_count: 2`, `reading_time_minutes: 1` ✅ |
| `POST` with spoofed `word_count: 99999, reading_time_minutes: 99999` and `"one two three"` | `word_count: 3`, `reading_time_minutes: 1` (spoofed values overwritten) ✅ |
| Superuser `admin@example.com` / `SuperSecret123` auth | Authenticates successfully ✅ |

**Server running at:** `http://0.0.0.0:8090`
